sections.paragraph: (“the well of the oath,” according to Gen 21:31 26:33 but see below[?]), [Beer-sheba], pr.n. of an ancient town situated on the southern border of Palestine ( 2Sa 24:7), whence the limits of the Holy Land are described, מִדּן עַד־בְּאֵר שֶׁבַע, Jud. 20:1 the limits of the kingdom of Judah are said to be מִגֶּבַע עַד־בְּאֵר שֶׁבַע 2Ki 23:8. At present Seetzen states that there are found there five or seven wells called Szabחa, with a valley of the same name (see Zach, Correspondenz, xvii. 141). [Robinson, i. 303.]
